name: The trial before the Sanhedrin
notes: The fourfold account of the Jewish trial of Jesus before the high priest. The synoptics give the night hearing before Caiaphas with the assembled council: the seeking of false witness, the temple-saying, the high priest's adjuration, Jesus' confession of the Son of man at the right hand of power, the rending of the clothes and charge of blasphemy, the sentence of death, and the mocking and smiting with the palms of their hands. John supplies the complementary vantage of the prior examination before Annas (father in law to Caiaphas) concerning Jesus' doctrine, the officer who struck him, and Annas sending him bound to Caiaphas; these are linked only where the wording genuinely overlaps, as at the leading away, Peter following into the palace, and the smiting. Luke sets the council's question and the confession in the morning sitting and omits the temple witness here. Where the accounts keep distinct detail or order they stand as complementary vantages, left as a matter for further study.
